As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "{{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|12=draft}}" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国|12=draft}}), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}). == Origins == === Historical Records === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} records that {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}.Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} of {{ill|815|ja|弘仁6年|12=draft}} states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=draft}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|12=draft}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} records that in {{ill|707|ja|慶雲4th year|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|12=draft}} in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft}} (121 meters) and {{ill|Biwasuka Kofun|ja|琵琶塚古墳|12=draft}} (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}}—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}}. == History == In {{ill|684|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|12=draft}}, Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft}}, who contributed to drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft}}, a poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft}} - Compiler of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft}} - Poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}} *{{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=draft}} *{{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=draft}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Imperial clans]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]]

{{Ancient Japanese Clans |氏名 = 下毛野氏 |image = [[File:下野薬師寺 復元回廊.JPG|280px]]
[[Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] |氏姓 = 下毛野君（公）
のち下毛野[[朝臣]] |始祖 = '''称'''・[[豊城入彦命]]
（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[Yamashiro Province ]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, a [[prince]] of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[10th century]], they were active as low-ranking military officers within the [[imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "{{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|12=draft}}" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国|12=draft}}), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}). == Origins == === Historical Records === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} records that {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}.Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} of {{ill|815|ja|弘仁6年|12=draft}} states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=draft}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|12=draft}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} records that in {{ill|707|ja|慶雲4th year|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|12=draft}} in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft}} (121 meters) and {{ill|Biwasuka Kofun|ja|琵琶塚古墳|12=draft}} (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}}—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}}. == History == In {{ill|684|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|12=draft}}, Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft}}, who contributed to drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft}}, a poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft}} - Compiler of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft}} - Poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}} *{{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=draft}} *{{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=draft}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Imperial clans]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]]

As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "{{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}}), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Origins == === Historical Records === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records that {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}.Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|815|ja|弘仁6年|12=draft|12=draft}} states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records that in {{ill|707|ja|慶雲4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft|12=draft}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (121 meters) and {{ill|Biwasuka Kofun|ja|琵琶塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == In {{ill|684|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, who contributed to drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, a poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} - Compiler of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft|12=draft}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} - Poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} *{{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=draft|12=draft}} *{{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsukeno clan}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Imperial clans]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]]

{{Ancient Japanese Clans |氏名 = 下毛野氏 |image = [[File:下野薬師寺 復元回廊.JPG|280px]]
[[Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] |氏姓 = 下毛野君（公）
のち下毛野[[朝臣]] |始祖 = '''称'''・[[豊城入彦命]]
（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[Yamashiro Province ]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, a [[prince]] of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[10th century]], they were active as low-ranking military officers within the [[imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "{{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}}), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Origins == === Historical Records === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records that {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}.Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|815|ja|弘仁6年|12=draft|12=draft}} states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records that in {{ill|707|ja|慶雲4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft|12=draft}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (121 meters) and {{ill|Biwasuka Kofun|ja|琵琶塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == In {{ill|684|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, who contributed to drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, a poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} - Compiler of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft|12=draft}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} - Poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} *{{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=draft|12=draft}} *{{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsukeno clan}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Imperial clans]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]]

（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[Yamashiro Province ]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, a [[prince]] of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[10th century]], they were active as low-Ranking military officers within the [[imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "{{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}}), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Origins == === Historical Records === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records that {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}.Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|815|ja|弘仁6年|12=draft|12=draft}} states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records that in {{ill|707|ja|慶雲4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft|12=draft}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (121 meters) and {{ill|Biwasuka Kofun|ja|琵琶塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == In {{ill|684|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, who contributed to drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, a poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} - Compiler of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft|12=draft}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} - Poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsukeno clan}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Imperial clans]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]]

The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records that in {{ill|707|ja|慶雲4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft|12=draft}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (121 meters) and {{ill|Biwasuka Kofun|ja|琵琶塚古墳|1=Biwazuka Kofun|2=ja|3=琵琶塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == In {{ill|684|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, who contributed to drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, a poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} - Compiler of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft|12=draft}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} - Poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsukeno clan}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Imperial clans]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]]

{{Ancient Japanese Clans |氏名 = 下毛野氏 |image = [[File:下野薬師寺 復元回廊.JPG|280px]]
[[Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] |氏姓 = 下毛野君（公）
のち下毛野[[朝臣]] |始祖 = '''称'''・[[豊城入彦命]]
（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[Yamashiro Province ]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, a [[prince]] of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[10th century]], they were active as low-Ranking military officers within the [[imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "{{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}}), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|12=draft|12=draft}}" (later {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Origins == === Historical Records === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records that {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}.Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|815|ja|弘仁6年|12=draft|12=draft}} states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} records that in {{ill|707|ja|慶雲4th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft|12=draft}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (121 meters) and {{ill|Biwasuka Kofun|ja|琵琶塚古墳|1=Biwazuka Kofun|2=ja|3=琵琶塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == In {{ill|684|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=draft|12=draft}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, who contributed to drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, a poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} - Compiler of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft|12=draft}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} - Poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsukeno clan}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Imperial clans]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]]

As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "{{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (later {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (later {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Origins == === Historical Records === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|815|ja|弘仁6年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that in {{ill|707|ja|慶雲4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (121 meters) and {{ill|Biwasuka Kofun|ja|琵琶塚古墳|1=Biwazuka Kofun|2=ja|3=琵琶塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == In {{ill|684|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who contributed to drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Compiler of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsukeno clan}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Imperial clans]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]]

revision.comment: 28 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:下毛野氏 up to rev 90631079
revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 下毛野氏 |画像 = [[ファイル:下野薬師寺 復元回廊.JPG|280px]]
[[氏寺]]とした[[下野薬師寺跡]]（[[栃木県]][[下野市]]） |氏姓 = 下毛野君（公）
のち下毛野[[朝臣]] |始祖 = '''称'''・[[豊城入彦命]]
（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[山城国]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''下毛野氏'''（しもつけのうじ、しもつけぬうじ江戸時代から近年まで、「しもつけの」は「しもつけぬ」とも呼ばれた。現在は「しもつけの」が正しい読みとされる（「[[毛野#「毛野」の由来と読み]]」参照）。）は、「下毛野」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。 第10代[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]の[[豊城入彦命]]の後裔を称する[[皇別]][[氏族]]で、「下毛野君（公）」のち「下毛野[[朝臣]]」姓を称した。 == 概要 == [[大化]]以後に中級貴族、[[10世紀]]以後は公家の下級武官として活躍した氏族である。国史には当初より朝廷に仕えた氏族として記載されており、大化以前の動向は明らかでない{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏}}。名称の「下毛野」に見えるように、元々は下毛野地域（現・[[栃木県]]南西部）における[[豪族]]であったと推測されているが、記載が少なく定かでない。 名称にある「[[毛野]]」とは古代の群馬県・栃木県南西部を指す地域名称で{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1985|p=|loc=§. 毛野国}}、都に近い側を「上毛野」（のち[[上野国]]（現・[[群馬県]]））、遠い側を「下毛野」（のち那須を加えて[[下野国]]（現・栃木県））としたのが基となっている。 == 出自 == === 文献上の記載 === 『[[日本書紀]]』には第10代[[崇神天皇]]が[[皇子]]の[[豊城入彦命|豊城命]]（豊城入彦命/豊木入日子命）に東国統治を命じたと記載するが、その豊城命について「上毛野君・下毛野君の祖」であると付記している『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年条。。なお豊城入彦命がそれらの祖である旨は、『[[古事記]]』にも記されている『古事記』崇神天皇記。。 [[平安時代]]初期の[[弘仁]]6年（[[815年]]）『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には下毛野朝臣について「崇神天皇皇子の豊城入彦命の後」と記載されており『新撰姓氏録』左京 皇別 下毛野朝臣条。、豊城入彦命の後裔であることが公称されていた。なお『新撰姓氏録』に見られるように豊城入彦命の後裔を称する氏族は他にも多くあるが、中でも下毛野朝臣のほか[[上毛野氏|上毛野朝臣]]、大野朝臣、池田朝臣、佐味朝臣、車持朝臣の6氏族は「[[東国六腹朝臣]]」{{Efn2|『続日本紀』延暦10年4月条にその呼称が見られ、これら6氏族に比定される{{Sfn|熊倉|近藤|2001|p={{要ページ番号|date=2018年12月}}}}。}}と総称され、ともに[[毛野]]地域（[[群馬県]]・栃木県南西部）出身で出自を同じくするという。 『[[続日本紀]]』には[[慶雲]]4年（[[707年]]）に[[下毛野石代]]が姓を「下毛野朝臣」から「下毛野川内朝臣」に変えたという記載があり『続日本紀』慶雲4年3月22日条。、下野国[[河内郡]]との関係が指摘されている{{Sfn|熊倉|2008|p=|loc=§. 第三章のうち「下毛野君の本拠地」節}}。 また疑いも多いが、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」では、[[仁徳天皇]]の御世に豊城命四世孫の[[奈良別]]が初めて[[下毛野国造]]に任じられたと記されている『国造本紀』下毛野国造条。。奈良別については「六世孫の下毛君奈良」と記載する文献もあり『新撰姓氏録』左京 皇別 大網公条。、下毛野氏一族とも推定されている。 === 考古資料による考証 === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[吾妻古墳]]（[[栃木県]][[栃木市]]・[[壬生町]]）
下野型古墳で、下毛野氏との関係が指摘される。]] 毛野地域（群馬県・栃木県南西部）には[[古墳時代]]に多くの[[古墳]]が築かれたが、下毛野地域においては5世紀末から6世紀に[[小山市]]北部において最大規模の摩利支天塚古墳（墳丘長121メートル）と琵琶塚古墳（123メートル）が築かれた{{Sfn|福田|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}}。 代わってやや北方の[[壬生町]]南部に、両古墳とは性格の異なる「下野型古墳」と呼ばれる独特の[[前方後円墳]]群が築かれた{{Efn2|特徴として、石室が後円部でなく前方部にあり、墳丘が基壇の上に造られている。例として、吾妻古墳（栃木市壬生町、墳丘長約86メートル）、国分寺愛宕塚古墳（壬生町、約75メートル）、茶臼山古墳（壬生町、約75メートル）、山王塚古墳（下野市、約50メートル）がある{{Sfn|山本|2013|p=|loc=§. Q4}}。}}{{Sfn|福田|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}}。これら下野型古墳が下毛野氏一族の墓と解されている『栃木県の歴史』 山川出版社、{{Full citation needed|date=2018年12月}}、1章 3節「下毛野の王者」。。また、古墳群は山王塚古墳（6世紀末から7世紀初頭）まで続いたのち、円墳に変化することから、この頃にヤマト王権の東国支配が完了し下毛野氏一族は中央に居を移したと指摘される{{Sfn|福田|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}}。 古墳群北東の[[下野薬師寺跡|下野薬師寺]]は[[下毛野古麻呂]]による[[氏寺]]としての建立と伝わることからも「[[下野薬師寺跡]]」参照。、当地と下毛野氏の関係の深さがうかがわれる。また、古墳群付近には[[下野国庁跡|下野国庁]]や[[下野国分寺跡|下野国分寺]]・国分尼寺も建立されており、一帯はのちの[[下野国]]においても中心地であった。 == 歴史 == [[飛鳥時代]]以降の歴史としは、[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）11月で、[[八色の姓]]において他の52氏とともに「下毛野君」に[[朝臣]]の姓が授けられたとされる『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月条。 一族としては[[下毛野古麻呂]]が特に名高く、『[[大宝律令]]』撰定に従事したことが知られる。また、大学助教であった[[下毛野虫麻呂]]が『[[懐風藻]]』に[[漢詩]]を残しているように、学問的活躍が目立っている{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏}}。 しかしながら以後は中央の中・下級貴族にとどまり{{Sfn|平凡社|1988|p=|loc=§. 下野国}}、[[10世紀]]以降は[[近衛府]]の[[舎人]]や院・[[摂家|摂関家]]の[[随身]]としての活躍が見られる{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}}。摂関家とは[[藤原道長]]の頃から結びつきを強め、家人として供奉・警固の任にあたった{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}}。その関係で馬術・鷹飼・舞楽・調理に優れ、厩所・随身所・雑色所の長を務め、荘園の管理も行なっていた{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}}。 その後も[[山城国]][[乙訓郡]]の調子荘を本領として任にあたっていたが、[[室町時代]]中期以降衰退したという{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}}。 == 主な下毛野氏 == === 人物 === * [[下毛野古麻呂]] - [[正四位|正四位下]]、[[式部省|式部卿]]大将軍。『[[大宝律令]]』撰定に従事。[[下野薬師寺]]を[[氏寺]]として建立したという。 * [[下毛野石代]] - [[従五位|従五位下]]。下毛野川内朝臣に改姓。また征夷副将軍に任じられる。 * [[下毛野虫麻呂]] - [[大学寮|大学助教]]。[[式部省|式部員外少輔]]。『[[懐風藻]]』に漢詩が残る。 * [[下毛野多具比]] - [[従五位|従五位上]]、[[遠江国|遠江守]]。 * [[下毛野稻麻呂]] - [[従四位|従四位下]]。 * [[下毛野公時]] - 近衛[[舎人]]、[[番長#番長（律令官制）|番長]]、[[藤原道長]]の[[随身]]。[[童話]]「[[金太郎]]」のモデルとなったとされる{{Sfn|川島|1992|pp=76-97}}。 '''他氏出身''' * [[下毛野根麻呂]] - [[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]。吉弥侯のち下毛野公。 === 氏族・家 === [[近衛府|左右近衛府]][[地下人|地下官人]]の[[調子家]]・[[富家]]が下毛野姓を称し、[[江戸時代]]を通じて存続した。 また、[[宇都宮氏]]など下野地域の社家・武家に後裔と推測される氏族がある。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''原典''' {{Reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|editor-last=上田|editor-first=正昭|editor-link=上田正昭|others=[[井上満郎]]; [[愛宕元]]; [[西谷正]]; [[和田萃]]編集委員|chapter=§. 下毛野氏|date=2006-1|title=日本古代史大辞典 : 旧石器時代〜鎌倉幕府成立頃|publisher=[[大和書房]]|isbn=4479840656|ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=川島茂裕|date=1992-12|title=下毛野公時と金太郎伝説の成立|journal=国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告|issue=第45集 田中稔教授追悼号|editor=[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]|publisher=国立歴史民俗博物館|pages=79-97|ncid=AN00377607|naid=40004437135|ref={{SfnRef|川島|1992|}}}} * {{Citation|和書|last=熊倉|first=浩靖|last2=近藤|first2=義雄|author-link=|contribution=上毛野国から東国へ|edition=|date=2001-11|title=群馬史再発見|publisher=あさを社|isbn=4870243334|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|和書|last=熊倉|first=浩靖|author-link=|date=2008-2|edition=改訂増補|title=古代東国の王者 : 上毛野氏の研究|publisher=[[雄山閣]]|isbn=9784639020073|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|和書|last=|first=|author-link=|contribution=毛野国|date=1985|volume=第5|editor=国史大辞典編集委員会|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=4-642-00505-6|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|和書|last=|first=|author-link=|contribution=下毛野氏|date=1986-11|volume=第7|editor=国史大辞典編集委員会|title=国史大辞典|publisher=吉川弘文館|isbn=4-642-00507-2|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/下毛野氏|title=下毛野氏|publisher=[[コトバンク]]|author=世界大百科事典|accessdate=2018年12月23日|ref={{SfnRef|kotobank-下毛野氏}}}} * {{Citation|和書|last=|first=|author-link=|contribution=下毛野氏|edition=|date=|volume=|editor=|title=世界大百科事典|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|和書|last=福田|first=三男|author-link=|date=1998-9|title=光と風の大地から : 下野古麻呂の生涯|publisher=[[随想舎]]|isbn=4887480121|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=平凡社地方資料センター編|date=1988-8|title=栃木県の地名|series=日本歴史地名大系 9|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093||ref={{SfnRef|平凡社|1988}}}} * {{Citation|和書|author=[[山本博文]]監修|date=2013-1|title=あなたの知らない栃木県の歴史|series=歴史新書|publisher=[[洋泉社]]|isbn=9784800300720|ref={{SfnRef|山本|2013|}}}} * {{Citation|和書|last=|first=|author-link=|date={{Full citation needed|date=2018年12月}}|title=栃木県の歴史|series=|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|isbn=|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[東国六腹朝臣]] * [[下毛野国造]] * [[宇都宮二荒山神社]] * [[下野薬師寺跡]] - 当初は下毛野氏の氏寺。 {{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけのうし}} revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[下毛野氏]] to [[Shimotsukeno clan]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 下毛野氏 |画像 = [[ファイル:下野薬師寺 復元回廊.JPG|280px]]
[[氏寺]]とした[[下野薬師寺跡]]（[[栃木県]][[下野市]]） |氏姓 = 下毛野君（公）
のち下毛野[[朝臣]] |始祖 = '''称'''・[[豊城入彦命]]
（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[山城国]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''下毛野氏'''（しもつけのうじ、しもつけぬうじ江戸時代から近年まで、「しもつけの」は「しもつけぬ」とも呼ばれた。現在は「しもつけの」が正しい読みとされる（「[[毛野#「毛野」の由来と読み]]」参照）。）は、「下毛野」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。 第10代[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]の[[豊城入彦命]]の後裔を称する[[皇別]][[氏族]]で、「下毛野君（公）」のち「下毛野[[朝臣]]」姓を称した。 == 概要 == [[大化]]以後に中級貴族、[[10世紀]]以後は公家の下級武官として活躍した氏族である。国史には当初より朝廷に仕えた氏族として記載されており、大化以前の動向は明らかでない{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏}}。名称の「下毛野」に見えるように、元々は下毛野地域（現・[[栃木県]]南西部）における[[豪族]]であったと推測されているが、記載が少なく定かでない。 名称にある「[[毛野]]」とは古代の群馬県・栃木県南西部を指す地域名称で{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1985|p=|loc=§. 毛野国}}、都に近い側を「上毛野」（のち[[上野国]]（現・[[群馬県]]））、遠い側を「下毛野」（のち那須を加えて[[下野国]]（現・栃木県））としたのが基となっている。 == 出自 == === 文献上の記載 === 『[[日本書紀]]』には第10代[[崇神天皇]]が[[皇子]]の[[豊城入彦命|豊城命]]（豊城入彦命/豊木入日子命）に東国統治を命じたと記載するが、その豊城命について「上毛野君・下毛野君の祖」であると付記している『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年条。。なお豊城入彦命がそれらの祖である旨は、『[[古事記]]』にも記されている『古事記』崇神天皇記。。 [[平安時代]]初期の[[弘仁]]6年（[[815年]]）『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には下毛野朝臣について「崇神天皇皇子の豊城入彦命の後」と記載されており『新撰姓氏録』左京 皇別 下毛野朝臣条。、豊城入彦命の後裔であることが公称されていた。なお『新撰姓氏録』に見られるように豊城入彦命の後裔を称する氏族は他にも多くあるが、中でも下毛野朝臣のほか[[上毛野氏|上毛野朝臣]]、大野朝臣、池田朝臣、佐味朝臣、車持朝臣の6氏族は「[[東国六腹朝臣]]」{{Efn2|『続日本紀』延暦10年4月条にその呼称が見られ、これら6氏族に比定される{{Sfn|熊倉|近藤|2001|p={{要ページ番号|date=2018年12月}}}}。}}と総称され、ともに[[毛野]]地域（[[群馬県]]・栃木県南西部）出身で出自を同じくするという。 『[[続日本紀]]』には[[慶雲]]4年（[[707年]]）に[[下毛野石代]]が姓を「下毛野朝臣」から「下毛野川内朝臣」に変えたという記載があり『続日本紀』慶雲4年3月22日条。、下野国[[河内郡]]との関係が指摘されている{{Sfn|熊倉|2008|p=|loc=§. 第三章のうち「下毛野君の本拠地」節}}。 また疑いも多いが、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」では、[[仁徳天皇]]の御世に豊城命四世孫の[[奈良別]]が初めて[[下毛野国造]]に任じられたと記されている『国造本紀』下毛野国造条。。奈良別については「六世孫の下毛君奈良」と記載する文献もあり『新撰姓氏録』左京 皇別 大網公条。、下毛野氏一族とも推定されている。 === 考古資料による考証 === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[吾妻古墳]]（[[栃木県]][[栃木市]]・[[壬生町]]）
下野型古墳で、下毛野氏との関係が指摘される。]] 毛野地域（群馬県・栃木県南西部）には[[古墳時代]]に多くの[[古墳]]が築かれたが、下毛野地域においては5世紀末から6世紀に[[小山市]]北部において最大規模の摩利支天塚古墳（墳丘長121メートル）と琵琶塚古墳（123メートル）が築かれた{{Sfn|福田|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}}。 代わってやや北方の[[壬生町]]南部に、両古墳とは性格の異なる「下野型古墳」と呼ばれる独特の[[前方後円墳]]群が築かれた{{Efn2|特徴として、石室が後円部でなく前方部にあり、墳丘が基壇の上に造られている。例として、吾妻古墳（栃木市壬生町、墳丘長約86メートル）、国分寺愛宕塚古墳（壬生町、約75メートル）、茶臼山古墳（壬生町、約75メートル）、山王塚古墳（下野市、約50メートル）がある{{Sfn|山本|2013|p=|loc=§. Q4}}。}}{{Sfn|福田|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}}。これら下野型古墳が下毛野氏一族の墓と解されている『栃木県の歴史』 山川出版社、{{Full citation needed|date=2018年12月}}、1章 3節「下毛野の王者」。。また、古墳群は山王塚古墳（6世紀末から7世紀初頭）まで続いたのち、円墳に変化することから、この頃にヤマト王権の東国支配が完了し下毛野氏一族は中央に居を移したと指摘される{{Sfn|福田|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}}。 古墳群北東の[[下野薬師寺跡|下野薬師寺]]は[[下毛野古麻呂]]による[[氏寺]]としての建立と伝わることからも「[[下野薬師寺跡]]」参照。、当地と下毛野氏の関係の深さがうかがわれる。また、古墳群付近には[[下野国庁跡|下野国庁]]や[[下野国分寺跡|下野国分寺]]・国分尼寺も建立されており、一帯はのちの[[下野国]]においても中心地であった。 == 歴史 == [[飛鳥時代]]以降の歴史としは、[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）11月で、[[八色の姓]]において他の52氏とともに「下毛野君」に[[朝臣]]の姓が授けられたとされる『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月条。 一族としては[[下毛野古麻呂]]が特に名高く、『[[大宝律令]]』撰定に従事したことが知られる。また、大学助教であった[[下毛野虫麻呂]]が『[[懐風藻]]』に[[漢詩]]を残しているように、学問的活躍が目立っている{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏}}。 しかしながら以後は中央の中・下級貴族にとどまり{{Sfn|平凡社|1988|p=|loc=§. 下野国}}、[[10世紀]]以降は[[近衛府]]の[[舎人]]や院・[[摂家|摂関家]]の[[随身]]としての活躍が見られる{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}}。摂関家とは[[藤原道長]]の頃から結びつきを強め、家人として供奉・警固の任にあたった{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}}。その関係で馬術・鷹飼・舞楽・調理に優れ、厩所・随身所・雑色所の長を務め、荘園の管理も行なっていた{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}}。 その後も[[山城国]][[乙訓郡]]の調子荘を本領として任にあたっていたが、[[室町時代]]中期以降衰退したという{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}}。 == 主な下毛野氏 == === 人物 === * [[下毛野古麻呂]] - [[正四位|正四位下]]、[[式部省|式部卿]]大将軍。『[[大宝律令]]』撰定に従事。[[下野薬師寺]]を[[氏寺]]として建立したという。 * [[下毛野石代]] - [[従五位|従五位下]]。下毛野川内朝臣に改姓。また征夷副将軍に任じられる。 * [[下毛野虫麻呂]] - [[大学寮|大学助教]]。[[式部省|式部員外少輔]]。『[[懐風藻]]』に漢詩が残る。 * [[下毛野多具比]] - [[従五位|従五位上]]、[[遠江国|遠江守]]。 * [[下毛野稻麻呂]] - [[従四位|従四位下]]。 * [[下毛野公時]] - 近衛[[舎人]]、[[番長#番長（律令官制）|番長]]、[[藤原道長]]の[[随身]]。[[童話]]「[[金太郎]]」のモデルとなったとされる{{Sfn|川島|1992|pp=76-97}}。 '''他氏出身''' * [[下毛野根麻呂]] - [[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]。吉弥侯のち下毛野公。 === 氏族・家 === [[近衛府|左右近衛府]][[地下人|地下官人]]の[[調子家]]・[[富家]]が下毛野姓を称し、[[江戸時代]]を通じて存続した。 また、[[宇都宮氏]]など下野地域の社家・武家に後裔と推測される氏族がある。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = 下毛野氏 |image = [[File:下野薬師寺 復元回廊.JPG|280px]]
[[Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] |氏姓 = 下毛野君（公）
のち下毛野[[朝臣]] |始祖 = '''称'''・[[豊城入彦命]]
（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[Yamashiro Province ]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a [[prince]] of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[10th century]], they were active as low-Ranking military officers within the [[imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "{{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (later {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (later {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Origins == === Historical Records === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|815|ja|弘仁6年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that in {{ill|707|ja|慶雲4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (121 meters) and {{ill|Biwasuka Kofun|ja|琵琶塚古墳|1=Biwazuka Kofun|2=ja|3=琵琶塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == In {{ill|684|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who contributed to drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Compiler of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsukeno clan}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Imperial clans]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]] {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = 下毛野氏 |image = [[File:下野薬師寺 復元回廊.JPG|280px]]
[[Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] |氏姓 = 下毛野君（公）
のち下毛野[[朝臣]] |始祖 = '''称'''・[[豊城入彦命]]
（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[Yamashiro Province ]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a [[prince]] of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[10th century]], they were active as low-Ranking military officers within the [[imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "{{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (later {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (later {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Origins == === Historical Records === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|815|ja|弘仁6年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that in {{ill|707|ja|慶雲4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (121 meters) and {{ill|Biwasuka Kofun|ja|琵琶塚古墳|1=Biwazuka Kofun|2=ja|3=琵琶塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == In {{ill|684|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who contributed to drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Compiler of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsukeno clan}} [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]]

As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "{{ill|Keno|ja|毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (later {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (later {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Origins == === Historical Records === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|815|ja|弘仁6年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that in {{ill|707|ja|慶雲4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|{{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (121 meters) and {{ill|Biwasuka Kofun|ja|琵琶塚古墳|1=Biwazuka Kofun|2=ja|3=琵琶塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == In {{ill|684|ja|Emperor Tenmu13rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who contributed to drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Compiler of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Poet featured in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]]
