のち[[Yamashiro Province ]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[Kōbetsu|Imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[:en:Taika Reforms|Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[:en:10th century|10th century]], they were active as low-Ranking military officers within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "[[:en:Keno Province|Keno]]" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). == Origins == === Historical Records === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] of [[:en:815|815]] states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi clan|qq=}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|qq=}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records that in [[:en:707|707]], {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as [[:en:Marishitenzuka Kofun|Marishitenzuka Kofun]] (121 meters) and [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]] (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]]—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins]]. == History == In [[:en:684|684]], Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, who contributed to drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}}, a poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]].{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} - Compiler of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} - Poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[sn:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[sn:himotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q16142940}}

Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi clan|qq=}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|qq=}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records that in [[:en:707|707]], {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as [[:en:Marishitenzuka Kofun|Marishitenzuka Kofun]] (121 meters) and [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]] (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]]—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins]]. == History == In [[:en:684|684]], Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, who contributed to drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}}, a poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]].{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} - Compiler of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} - Poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q16142940}}

[[ja:下毛野氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q16142940]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = 下毛野氏 |image = [[File:下野薬師寺 復元回廊.JPG|280px]]
[[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] |氏姓 = 下毛野君（公）
のち下毛野[[朝臣]] |始祖 = '''称'''・[[豊城入彦命]]
（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[Yamashiro Province ]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[Kōbetsu|Imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[:en:Taika Reforms|Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[:en:10th century|10th century]], they were active as low-Ranking military officers within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "[[:en:Keno Province|Keno]]" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). == Origins == === Historical Records === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] of [[:en:815|815]] states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi clan|qq=}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|qq=}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records that in [[:en:707|707]], {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as [[:en:Marishitenzuka Kofun|Marishitenzuka Kofun]] (121 meters) and [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]] (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]]—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins]]. == History == In [[:en:684|684]], Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, who contributed to drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}}, a poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]].{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} - Compiler of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} - Poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q16142940}}

Poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q16142940}}

[[ja:下毛野氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q16142940]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = 下毛野氏 |image = [[File:下野薬師寺 復元回廊.JPG|280px]]
[[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] |氏姓 = 下毛野君（公）
のち下毛野[[朝臣]] |始祖 = '''称'''・[[豊城入彦命]]
（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[Yamashiro Province ]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[Kōbetsu|Imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[:en:Taika Reforms|Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[:en:10th century|10th century]], they were active as low-Ranking military officers within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "[[:en:Keno Province|Keno]]" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). == Origins == === Historical Records === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] of [[:en:815|815]] states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi clan|qq=}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|qq=}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records that in [[:en:707|707]], {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as [[:en:Marishitenzuka Kofun|Marishitenzuka Kofun]] (121 meters) and [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]] (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]]—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins]]. == History == In [[:en:684|684]], Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, who contributed to drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}}, a poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]].{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} - Compiler of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} - Poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q16142940}}

The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[:en:Taika Reforms|Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[:en:10th century|10th century]], they were active as low-Ranking military officers within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "[[:en:Keno Province|Keno]]" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). == Origins == === Historical Records === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] of [[:en:815|815]] states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi clan|qq=}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|qq=}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records that in [[:en:707|707]], {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as [[:en:Marishitenzuka Kofun|Marishitenzuka Kofun]] (121 meters) and [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]] (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]]—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins]]. == History == In [[:en:684|684]], Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, who contributed to drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}}, a poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]].{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} - Compiler of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} - Poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q16142940}}

This [[Kōbetsu|Imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[:en:Taika Reforms|Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "[[:en:Keno Province|Keno]]" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). == Origins == === Historical Records === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] of [[:en:815|815]] states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi clan|qq=}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|qq=}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records that in [[:en:707|707]], {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as [[:en:Marishitenzuka Kofun|Marishitenzuka Kofun]] (121 meters) and [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]] (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]]—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins]]. == History == In [[:en:684|684]], Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, who contributed to drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}}, a poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]].{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} - Compiler of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} - Poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q16142940}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:下毛野氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q16142940]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = 下毛野氏 |image = [[File:下野薬師寺 復元回廊.JPG|280px]]
[[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] |氏姓 = 下毛野君（公）
のち下毛野[[朝臣]] |始祖 = '''称'''・[[豊城入彦命]]
（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[Yamashiro Province ]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[Kōbetsu|Imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[:en:Taika Reforms|Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[:en:10th century|10th century]], they were active as low-Ranking military officers within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "[[:en:Keno Province|Keno]]" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). == Origins == === Historical Records === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] of [[:en:815|815]] states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi clan|qq=}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|qq=}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records that in [[:en:707|707]], {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as [[:en:Marishitenzuka Kofun|Marishitenzuka Kofun]] (121 meters) and [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]] (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]]—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins]]. == History == In [[:en:684|684]], Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, who contributed to drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}}, a poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]].{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} - Compiler of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} - Poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q16142940}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[Kōbetsu|Imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[:en:Taika Reforms|Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[:en:10th century|10th century]], they were active as low-Ranking military officers within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "[[:en:Keno Province|Keno]]" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). == Origins == === Historical Records === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] of [[:en:815|815]] states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi clan|qq=}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|qq=}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records that in [[:en:707|707]], {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as [[:en:Marishitenzuka Kofun|Marishitenzuka Kofun]] (121 meters) and [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]] (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]]—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins]]. == History == In [[:en:684|684]], Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, who contributed to drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}}, a poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]].{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} - Compiler of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} - Poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q16142940}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi clan|qq=}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|qq=}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records that in [[:en:707|707]], {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as [[:en:Marishitenzuka Kofun|Marishitenzuka Kofun]] (121 meters) and [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]] (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]]—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins]]. == History == In [[:en:684|684]], Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, who contributed to drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}}, a poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]].{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} - Compiler of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} - Poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q16142940}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[ja:下毛野氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q16142940]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = 下毛野氏 |image = [[File:下野薬師寺 復元回廊.JPG|280px]]
[[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] |氏姓 = 下毛野君（公）
のち下毛野[[朝臣]] |始祖 = '''称'''・[[豊城入彦命]]
（[[崇神天皇]][[皇子]]） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = 下毛野（現・栃木県南西部）?
のち[[Yamashiro Province ]]左京 |人物 = [[下毛野古麻呂]] |後裔 = '''[[調子家]]'''（[[地下家]]）
[[富家]]（[[地下家]]）など }} '''Shimotsukeno clan''' (下毛野氏, also read as ''Shimotsukenu'')While the reading ''Shimotsukenu'' has been used since the Edo period, some scholars argue this is a misreading. See [[Kenō#Origin and Pronunciation]]. is a [[Japanese clans|Japanese clan]] that derives its name from the region of Shimotsukeno. This [[Kōbetsu|Imperial clan]] (皇別氏族) claimed descent from {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a [[Imperial Prince|Prince]] of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The clan initially used the title "Shimotsukeno no Kimi" (下毛野君) or "Shimotsukeno no Kō" (下毛野公), later adopting the title "Shimotsukeno no Ason" (下毛野朝臣). == Overview == After the [[:en:Taika Reforms|Taika Reforms]], the Shimotsukeno clan became a mid-tier noble family. By the [[:en:10th century|10th century]], they were active as low-Ranking military officers within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]]. The clan is mentioned in early chronicles as serving the court, but their activities before the Taika Reforms remain unclear. As their name implies, the clan originated in the Shimotsukeno region (modern-day southwestern [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]), though historical evidence is sparse. The name "[[:en:Keno Province|Keno]]" refers to an ancient region encompassing modern-day [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] and southwestern Tochigi Prefecture. The area closer to the capital was called "{{ill|Kamitsukeno|ja|上毛野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]]), while the more distant part was termed "{{ill|Shimotsukeno|ja|下毛野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (later [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). == Origins == === Historical Records === The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] records that [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] ordered his son {{ill|Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} to govern the eastern provinces. It also identifies Toyokiiribiko as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno clan.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin, year 48. This lineage is similarly recorded in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].Kojiki, Emperor Sujin's record. The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] of [[:en:815|815]] states that the Shimotsukeno no Ason descended from Toyokiiribiko.Shinsen Shōjiroku, Sakyo Imperial Lineage, Shimotsukeno no Ason. Other clans claiming descent from Toyokiiribiko include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōno clan|ja|大野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuramochi clan|ja|車持氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi clan|qq=}}, collectively known as the {{ill|Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|ja|東国六腹朝臣|1=Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|2=ja|3=東国六腹朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgoku Six Clan Ason|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Aristocratic Clans of the Eastern Provinces|qq=}}.Shoku Nihongi, Enryaku 10, 4th month. The [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] records that in [[:en:707|707]], {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} changed the clan name to "Shimotsukeno Kawachi no Ason," indicating a connection to the Kawachi District.Shoku Nihongi, Keiun 4, 3rd month, 22nd day. === Archaeological Evidence === [[File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|210px|right|[[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] in Tochigi Prefecture.]] Numerous [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns were constructed in the Keno region during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In the Shimotsukeno area, the late 5th and early 6th centuries saw the construction of large kofun such as [[:en:Marishitenzuka Kofun|Marishitenzuka Kofun]] (121 meters) and [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]] (123 meters).{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} The unique "Shimotsuke-type Kofun," characterized by stone chambers in the anterior part and mounds on elevated platforms, are believed to be the tombs of the Shimotsukeno clan.{{Sfn|Fukuda|1998|p=|loc=§. 第三章 下野国成立}} Nearby, the ruins of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]]—the clan's temple—further demonstrate their historical significance.See [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins]]. == History == In [[:en:684|684]], Emperor Tenmu conferred the title "Ason" on the Shimotsukeno clan as part of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}.Nihon Shoki, Emperor Tenmu, year 13, 11th month. Notable members of the clan include {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, who contributed to drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], and {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}}, a poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]].{{Sfn|国史大辞典編集委員会|1986|p=|loc=§. 下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} Over time, the clan's influence waned, and they served as middle- and low-Ranking court officials.{{Sfn|kotobank-下毛野氏|loc=世界大百科事典}} == Notable Members == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} - Compiler of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}} - Deputy general in the conquest of the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} - Poet featured in the [[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] * [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=上野国|qq=Kōzuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国の式内社一覧|lt_ja=下野国|qq=Shimotsuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province|qq=}} == References == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary Sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Citations''' {{reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|下毛野氏|version=90631079|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 8]] [[ja:下毛野氏]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] {{wikidata link|Q16142940}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]
